project_information.project_outline:
Future Pathways Youth Employability Programme is a community-led initiative based in Glasgow that supports young people aged 16–24 who are furthest from the labour market to move into and sustain meaningful employment, training, or further education. We work primarily with young people who have left school with few or no qualifications, have experience of care, long-term unemployment, or mental health challenges, and who are not currently engaged in mainstream services.

The programme offers a rolling 16-week employability course that combines one-to-one coaching, small group workshops, and real-world work placements with local employers in sectors such as social care, construction, hospitality, and digital services. Participants receive tailored support to build core skills like communication, teamwork, timekeeping, and digital literacy, alongside practical help with CVs, interview preparation, and job search.

We also provide wraparound support including travel passes, lunch vouchers, and access to counselling and money advice. The project has been co-designed with local young people, and each cohort helps refine the content. Since launching in early 2020, Future Pathways has worked with over 90 young people, with 68% progressing into work, apprenticeships, college, or accredited training within six months of completing the programme.

main_contact_for_the_nominated_project.main_contact_address_line_1: Unit 4, Riverside Community Hub, 28 Clyde Street
